Milo found a locked gate at the end of the garden. He tried pushing and pulling, then noticed a latch hidden under ivy. Beyond the gate was a bench where his grandfather had left a book.<|endoftext|>
Ana's paper crown tore before the parade. She used two strips of tape on the inside and decorated the repair with silver stars. Nobody saw the tear, but everyone noticed the stars.<|endoftext|>
A duckling followed Jo across the farmyard. Jo walked slowly back to the pond and waited. The mother duck called, and the duckling hurried through the grass to join her.<|endoftext|>
Rina wanted to hear the sea inside a shell. The shell was quiet, so she held it near the wind. A soft rushing sound appeared, and Rina imagined waves under the moon.<|endoftext|>
Eli spilled blue paint on his picture. Instead of throwing it away, he turned the spot into a pond and added three frogs. The accident became his favorite part.<|endoftext|>
Mara and Sol built a bridge from sticks over a narrow stream. The first bridge bent, so they added short supports underneath. Their toy horse crossed without getting wet.<|endoftext|>
Theo heard thunder while walking home. He counted the seconds before each rumble and stayed beside his aunt. At home they watched the storm from a safe window.<|endoftext|>
Lena made a card for her teacher but spelled a word incorrectly. She covered it with a paper flower and wrote the word again. The teacher kept the card on her desk.<|endoftext|>
